tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'eyeglasses shops Kyoto'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has clusters of eyewear shops near temples — find the ones with modern frames and English-speaking staff before you land.
tasks.reward: First shopping day mapped out — walk off the plane and straight into buying 4 pairs
nuggets: Kyoto station area has 3+ optical chains
JINS and Zoff do same-day service
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto that can make prescription glasses quickly. I'm arriving March 23 and want to buy 4 pairs early in the trip (days 1-2). I need stores that: handle foreign prescriptions, speak some English, offer modern frame styles, and can do same-day or next-day service. Give me 5-7 specific store names with locations (Google Maps links), opening hours, and notes on selection/service speed. Prioritize areas near Kyoto Station and central Kyoto where I'll be staying.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance: subject 'Japan eyewear reimbursement — 4 pairs'
tasks.hook_line: 4 days out — if insurance won't cover foreign receipts, the whole glasses plan changes.
tasks.reward: Know exactly what receipts to collect and whether the $400+ savings is real
nuggets: Most US vision plans cover foreign purchases
Need itemized receipt with frame/lens breakdown
Prescription valid 1-2 years depending on state
tasks.system_prompt: Help me draft an email to my vision insurance provider asking about international eyewear purchase reimbursement. I'm traveling to Japan March 23-April 7 and planning to buy 4 pairs of prescription glasses there. I need to know: (1) Will they reimburse foreign purchases? (2) What documentation do I need (receipts, prescription format)? (3) Are there limits on number of pairs per year? (4) Do I need pre-approval? Also help me confirm if my current US prescription is sufficient or if I need anything converted to Japanese format. Keep the email concise and professional.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps, create new list 'Kyoto Must-See'
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — which ones are actually worth the early wake-up?
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left on the route
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free, open 24hrs, best dawn/dusk
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 2.5 days of activities in Kyoto (March 25-27). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, nature, and good food. Staying in central Kyoto near the station. I want a day-by-day itinerary with: temple names, opening hours, entry fees, Google Maps links, suggested walking routes connecting sites, and a mix of major landmarks (Fushimi Inari, Kinkaku-ji, Arashiyama) with lesser-known spots. Include breakfast/lunch/dinner recommendations near each area. Optimize for minimal backtracking and best light/crowd timing.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Takayama things to do March' and open top 3 results
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the trip's mountain interlude — old town streets, sake breweries, and alpine air before Tokyo chaos.
tasks.reward: Mountain day planned — gives the trip a breather between Kyoto temples and Tokyo energy
nuggets: Takayama old town walkable in 2-3 hours
Shirakawa-go day trip 50min bus, UNESCO village
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 1.5 days in Takayama (late March). Traveling with Dana. We like walking, nature, local food, and quieter experiences after busy Kyoto. Staying near Takayama station. I want: (1) walking route through old town with sake brewery recommendations, (2) best morning market to visit, (3) whether Shirakawa-go day trip is worth it (we like scenic villages and mountain views), (4) dinner spots for Hida beef. Include opening hours, travel times, and what to skip if short on time.

tasks.first_domino: List Tokyo neighborhoods on paper: Shibuya, Shinjuku, Asakusa, Harajuku
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is 8 days wide — which neighborhoods get your limited edition fountain pen hunt, and which get skipped?
tasks.reward: Trip fully planned end-to-end — nothing left but packing and boarding
nuggets: Itoya Ginza: 12 floors stationery, limited editions
Sailor Pop-Up Harajuku: exclusive Tokyo colors
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan 8 days in Tokyo (late March to early April). Traveling with Dana. We like: walking, food, stationery/fountain pens (I want to hunt limited edition Sailor pens), quirky neighborhoods, parks, and some nightlife. Staying near Shinjuku. I want: (1) neighborhood-by-day breakdown (Shibuya, Harajuku, Asakusa, Ginza, etc.), (2) specific fountain pen shops with limited edition notes (Kingdom Note, Itoya, Maruzen), (3) food recommendations per area, (4) 2-3 day trip options (Nikko, Kamakura, Mt Fuji area), (5) what to skip if we're templed-out from Kyoto. Include walking routes and travel times between areas.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open README.md
tasks.hook_line: Developer portals always have cursed local setup — can you get it running without asking for help?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all Cerebro work — turns a parked project into an active workstream
nuggets: Check if it needs Docker or bare Node
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running locally. It's a work project I haven't touched in a while. I need to: (1) understand the tech stack (likely Node/React), (2) identify prerequisites (Docker, databases, API keys), (3) follow setup docs and troubleshoot common issues, (4) verify it's actually working with a test page load. Walk me through the setup step-by-step, asking clarifying questions as needed. If setup docs are unclear or outdated, help me reverse-engineer from the codebase structure.

recommended_slots.reason: 4 days out, ramp-up energy, low activation — just search and browse eyeglasses shops to start the day
Quick errand that unblocks the glasses plan — email insurance now before work hours start
Kyoto is 6 days away and still blank — morning research energy fits this curiosity-driven planning
